items.title: MACC chief denies political interference in investigations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has reiterated that no political interference or directives have ever influenced the agency's investigations. He said the MACC does not accept such instructions as its operations are overseen by five independent monitoring bodies. Azam said the primary challenge facing the commission today is securing solid evidence through intelligence-based investigation (IBI) methods.

items.title: Petronas to negotiate oil purchase with Russia to secure national supply
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Petroliam Nasional Berhad (Petronas) will negotiate with Russia to purchase oil to ensure the country maintains sufficient fuel supplies. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said many countries in Europe and the United States, which previously sanctioned Russia, were now competing to purchase oil from the nation. Also present were Transport Minister Anthony Loke and Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud.

items.title: KTMB expands EMUPlus
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Keretapi Tanah Melayu Bhd (KTMB) has expanded its EMUPlus service to Klang, giving Klang Valley residents a more convenient, comfortable and practical direct journey to Ipoh starting this week. KTMB Group chief executive officer Datuk Azlan Shah Al Bakri said the route includes stops at Klang, Shah Alam, Subang Jaya, KL Sentral and Ipoh. "Commuters in Klang, Shah Alam and Subang Jaya can now board the EMUPlus closer to their areas, without having to rely entirely on major stations like KL Sentral," he said.

items.title: No fuel surcharge for Penang ferry services
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Penang Port Commission (SPPP) and Penang Port Sdn Bhd have decided against imposing a fuel surcharge on ferry services for the Pangkalan Raja Tun Uda (PRTU) and Pangkalan Sultan Abdul Halim (PSAH) routes. He said both SPPP and Penang Port remained committed to balancing operational sustainability with public interest. "SPPP will continue to play its role as a trade facilitator and port regulator by ensuring ferry services remain sustainable and resilient while supporting mobility and economic activities in Penang," he said.

items.title: MIC still ‘core’ member of BN, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today asserted that MIC is still a component member of the coalition, amid speculations over its status. “They are still core members of BN,” he told reporters when asked about MIC’s status after officiating at the AGM of BN ally, Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress at the World Trade Centre here. At its assembly last November, MIC delegates voted to leave BN.

items.title: Sudden lane change suspected in fatal Miri crash involving sports coupe and lorry
items.summary: MIRI, April 18 — A sudden lane change is believed to have caused a fatal accident along Jalan Miri Bypass yesterday morning, said Miri District police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah. He said the accident involved a two-door sports coupe driven by a 40-year-old local man and a lorry driven by a 34-year-old local man. The sports coupe, which was travelling from Permyjaya towards Miri Airport, is suspected to have suddenly changed lanes from the right to the left side of the road.

items.title: PM attends Pasir Puteh development meeting
items.summary: PASIR PUTEH: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim attended the 2026 Pasir Puteh district development meeting at the Pasir Puteh Land and District Office here today. Anwar, who is also the finance minister, arrived at 11.50am and was greeted by Kelantan Menteri Besar Datuk Mohd Nassuruddin Daud, together with several state executive councillors and senior state officials. The special meeting was also attended by Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar and Public Service Department director-general Tan Sri Wan Ahmad Dahlan Abdul Aziz.

items.title: Fahmi: Govt to streamline anti-scam efforts with new laws, joint agency session soon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — A special session to update all relevant stakeholders on current developments related to online scams will be held the soonest, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil had said today. “We have been working closely with the relevant stakeholders on any matters relating to scams and in fact our last meeting was held last week. “It isn’t just fraud, but all kinds of online scams be it investment scams, love scams and many various other types of scams that has been abusing official channels.

items.title: New Jakarta, Bangkok routes planned for Sultan Ismail Petra Airport, says Loke
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The government is looking to attract airlines to introduce new flight routes, including Jakarta and Bangkok, at Sultan Ismail Petra Airport to boost connectivity and draw more travellers, including those from southern Thailand. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative marks a new chapter for the airport, which, although classified as a domestic facility, is now capable of handling international flights following the RM440 million recent upgrade. "In the near future, the government plans to attract airlines to introduce new flight routes at the airport.

items.title: Elderly man killed in crash while heading for Friday prayers
items.summary: KAJANG: A 79-year-old man on his way to a mosque for Friday prayers with his grandson was killed after his motorcycle collided with a lorry in Jalan Hulu Langat yesterday. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Naazron Abdul Yusof said police received a report involving the Honda EX5 motorcycle and the lorry at 1.20pm. "The lorry driver was unaware of the motorcycle and struck the victim.

items.title: Search underway for student feared drowned at Pantai Balok
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Pahang Education Department is monitoring a case involving a 16-year-old student who is feared drowned after going missing while foraging for clams at Pantai Balok here yesterday evening. It was earlier reported that the Pahang Fire and Rescue Department received an emergency call at 6.02pm. Mohd Salahuddin said the mission involves 12 personnel, a Fire Rescue Tender (FRT), a four-wheel-drive vehicle, a lorry, and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) unit.

items.title: HRD Corp reset aims to boost governance, execution and accountability, says CEO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Human Resource Development Corporation (HRD Corp) has completed a sweeping organisational reset, marking a decisive shift towards execution, governance and delivery as it seeks to rebuild public confidence and strengthen Malaysia's workforce development ecosystem. HRD Corp chief executive Datuk Shamir Aziz said the organisation is now firmly aligned with the policy direction set by Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan, particularly in reinforcing governance standards and ensuring training systems remain credible and industry-relevant. We move forward with discipline, clarity and execution," he said.

items.title: Zahid backs Kimma entry into BN, but says other components must agree ‘100pc’ first
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today said he is open to accepting the Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress (Kimma) as a full member. He said Kimma supporters need to prove themselves to the BN supreme council and make a formal application. He said the BN must present a cohesive front.

items.title: Lorry driver held in Klang for smuggling 3,400 litres of diesel
items.summary: KLANG: Selangor Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry enforcement officers foiled an attempt by a lorry driver to flee with 3,400 litres of subsidised diesel early today. State ministry chief enforcement officer Mohd Hanizam Kechek said a driver in his 20s was detained along with a bonded lorry modified with an additional fuel tank capacity of 6,000 litres. "An inspection revealed six Intermediate Bulk Container (IBC) units, each with a 1,000-litre capacity, filled with fuel suspected to be diesel.

items.title: Kimma renews push for BN inclusion, raises worker shortage woes at AGM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress (Kimma) president Datuk Seri Syed Ibrahim Kader today renewed calls for the party to be formally accepted as a component of Barisan Nasional (BN), questioning the delay despite decades of loyalty to the coalition. But the question remains — why are we still not a component party?” he said. This is our country,” he said.

items.title: Petronas vessels among Malaysian ships awaiting Strait of Hormuz passage, says PM
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The remaining four Malaysian vessels are expected to take time before being allowed to pass through the Strait of Hormuz following the latest developments, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today. He said one Petronas vessel had already arrived in Pengerang, while another was nearing Malaysian waters. he said Iran had approved passage for seven Malaysian vessels, but one could not depart due to technical issues at the port.

items.title: Ministry detects 31 cases of foreign vehicles filling RON95 since April 1st
items.summary: Enforcement director-general Datuk Azman Adam said the lower fuel price in Malaysia compared with neighbouring countries was among the factors driving the offences. Also present was JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli. Meanwhile, Aedy Fadly said JPJ had issued 5,984 summons notices involving compounds totalling RM2.195 million under Vehicle Entry Permit (VEP) enforcement since July 1, 2025.

items.title: Multi-agency retreat to tackle online scams soon, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A multi-agency retreat to address online scams will be held in the near future to streamline legal, enforcement and coordination efforts across government bodies. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the initiative by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) aims to address gaps in understanding and coordination among agencies, particularly in tackling increasingly complex online fraud cases. He also said the initiative would cover all types of scams, including investment scams, love scams and job scams, which often exploit digital communication platforms.

items.title: Ready for orders on ministers’ salary cuts, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Cabinet ministers are ready to accept any orders on salary cuts, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. He said the matter was discussed, however it depends on Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s call. “I’m ready to accept any orders from the Prime Minister.

items.title: Proposal to retain 300-litre RON95 quota for media to be discussed, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A proposal to retain the 300-litre monthly Budi Madani RON95 (Budi95) fuel quota for media practitioners will be tabled at the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting next Tuesday. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the proposal, raised by the Gabungan Kelab Media Malaysia (GKMM), would be deliberated at the meeting, with a decision to be conveyed thereafter. Yesterday, GKMM urged the government to consider a special exemption for registered media practitioners to maintain the 300-litre monthly RON95 quota.

items.title: Refurbished Sultan Ismail Petra Airport to boost Asean links, says PM
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The refurbished Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) in Pengkalan Chepa is set to open direct links to key Asean hubs, boosting Kelantan's trade and tourism, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said the RM440 million airport would serve as a major economic catalyst for the state while strengthening its connectivity to the regional network. "LTSIP, a RM440 million airport, is set to become an economic catalyst and connect Kelantan to the regional network," he said at the opening ceremony.

items.title: Anwar: Diesel price hike affects 15pct, 85pct still on subsidy
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today acknowledged that about 15 per cent of the population has been affected by recent diesel price increases following the Middle East conflict, stressing that the government is doing its best to ease the burden. He said while a segment of the people continues to feel the effects, the remaining 85 per cent are still purchasing diesel at subsidised rates rather than market prices. "However, only 15 per cent are buying at market prices, while 85 per cent are still buying at subsidised rates," he said after officiating the new Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) terminal in Pengkalan Chepa today.

items.title: UN: Last year was deadliest for Rohingya refugees
items.summary: GENEVA, (Switzerland): Last year was the deadliest ever for Rohingya refugees fleeing by boat from Bangladesh or Myanmar across the Indian Ocean towards Indonesia and Malaysia, said the United Nations High Commissioner for Refugees (UNHCR) on Friday, reported German Press Agency (dpa). "More than 6,500 Rohingya attempted perilous sea crossings that year, with one in seven reported missing or dead — the highest mortality rate worldwide of any major route for refugee and migrant sea journeys," it said. More than 1.3 million Rohingya refugees currently live in Bangladesh, mostly in refugee camps.

items.title: Cabinet ready for pay cuts, says Fahmi
items.summary: Unity government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the matter is not new and has been discussed since the administration first took office. He said cabinet members are fully aware of the issue, adding that any decision ultimately rests with Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Fahmi, who is also communications minister, said ministers are ready to comply with any directive issued by the prime minister.

items.title: Economist: Strait of Hormuz reopening good news but too early to celebrate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Iran's move to reopen the Strait of Hormuz is welcome news, but it is too early to celebrate as the security situation remains fragile with elevated insurance costs, University of Nottingham Malaysia assistant professor of business economics Dr Tan Chee Meng said. Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi on Friday declared the strait "completely open" for all commercial vessels, linking it to the ceasefire in Lebanon. Assume that a US$500,000 cargo is insured with a deductible of US$5,000.

items.title: Fuel supply stable through June, Petronas says as tanker with one million barrels of crude oil from Iraq arrives in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — The Petronas vessel Ocean Thunder has arrived in Malaysia carrying one million barrels of crude oil from Basrah, Iraq. The shipment forms part of a broader supply network managed by the national oil company to ensure stable fuel availability nationwide amid a disruption in the Strait of Hormuz amid West Asia tensions. “Malaysia's fuel supply is drawn from multiple sources across Petronas' wider supply network.

items.title: High profit margins drive 'mobile store' diesel smuggling tactics
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Fuel smuggling syndicates have adopted a new modus operandi by using large tanker lorries as mobile storage units to consolidate subsidised diesel collected by smaller vehicles. These syndicates deploy dozens of small lorries, known as 'lori semut', to abuse the Subsidised Diesel Control Scheme (SKDS) fleet cards at petrol stations. The subsidised diesel, initially filled into one to three-tonne lorries, is then transferred into large tankers with capacities ranging from 19,000 to 34,000 litres.

items.title: ‘I’m home’: Khairy Jamaluddin returns to Umno
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Three years after his sacking, Khairy Jamaluddin has been readmitted into Umno under the Gagasan Rumah Bangsa initiative, alongside thousands of other former members and leaders. “I’m home,” Khairy wrote on his official Instagram account last night, seemingly as a reaction to his return to the party, shortly after an announcement was made by Umno secretary-general Datuk Seri Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki. Khairy also expressed gratitude to Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and the party’s supreme council for the decision.

items.title: 'Look at each other' philosophy: A model for shared prosperity
items.summary: Japan's engagement with Malaysia spans nearly 70 years, during which our nations have developed Malaysia's human and industrial capital. Many returned to strengthen Malaysia's industrial and technological sectors. Around 1,600 Japanese companies operate across manufacturing, technology, and services, with many expanding locally across Malaysia.

items.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire may end if no deal reached by Wednesday
items.summary: MARYLAND: US President Donald Trump said on Friday that he may end the ceasefire with Iran unless a long-term deal to end the war is agreed by Wednesday. "Maybe I won't extend it, but the blockade (on Iranian ports) is going to remain," Trump told reporters on Air Force One on his way back to Washington from Phoenix, Arizona. — REUTERS © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Sultan Ismail Petra Airport can handle up to 4 million passengers a year - Loke
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) can accommodate up to four million passengers annually. Transport Minister Anthony Loke described the airport as the most modern and aesthetically impressive domestic airport in the country. He said the terminal size has been expanded from 12,000sqm to 36,000sqm.

items.title: Sabah rice supply stabilises as state boosts production to 35,000 tonnes
items.summary: State Agriculture, Fisheries, and Food Industry Minister Datuk Jamawi Jaafar said the state's buffer stock is maintained at a level sufficient to last up to six months as a contingency against potential supply crises. "We must have stock to last six months. Jamawi said engagements with Padiberas Nasional Bhd (Bernas) and wholesalers confirmed that the situation has improved.

items.title: Airlines hike fares as jet fuel prices surge amid Iran war
items.summary: Jet fuel prices have soared from US$85 to US$90 per barrel to US$150 to US$200 per barrel in recent weeks, a financial hit for an industry where fuel accounts for up to a quarter of operating expenses. AIR FRANCE-KLM The airline group said it planned to increase long-haul ticket prices to address surging fuel costs, with cabin fares set to rise by 50 euros (US$58) per round trip. The group's Dutch arm KLM said on April 16 it would cancel 160 flights in Europe in the coming month due to rising fuel costs.

items.title: Investment fraud losses hit RM1.47b, police say victims trapped in fake profits before being hit with hidden fees
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Investment scams caused losses of RM1.47 billion last year, involving 9,603 cases, with the Royal Malaysia Police identifying five main methods, most using social media as the primary channel. Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said tactics uncovered included fake companies (clone firms), promises of quick high returns, Ponzi schemes, love scams, and bogus crypto platforms. Rusdi said non-existent investment cases rose sharply from 2024 to March this year, with 6,337 cases causing RM848.6 million losses in 2024, surging to 9,603 cases and RM1.47 billion in 2025.

items.title: Australia extends relaxed fuel standards to bolster supply
items.summary: SYDNEY: Australia extended an easing on fuel-quality standards until September, Energy Minister Chris Bowen said on Saturday, as the nation grapples with the impact of the Iran war on its fuel supply. "I've decided to extend the period of higher sulphur for petrol in Australia," Bowen said in televised remarks. Production of diesel, jet fuel and petrol at a fire-hit oil refinery owned by Viva Energy (VEA.AX) in Victoria, Australia's second-most-populous state, remained unchanged from Friday, Bowen said.

items.title: Kosovo, Bosnia troops prepared for Gaza peacekeeping role
items.summary: PRISTINA: Kosovo and Bosnia on Friday announced they were prepared to deploy small numbers of soldiers to Gaza as part of a peacekeeping force proposed by US President Donald Trump. Bosnia's three-member presidency had previously approved the potential deployment in January. Announced as part of a ceasefire agreement that halted two years of war, the planned International Stabilisation Force was proposed by the Trump-led "Board of Peace."

items.title: PM prays for Malaysian haj pilgrims' ease, good health in Holy Land
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim has prayed for Malaysian haj pilgrims to be granted ease in their affairs and good health as they carry out their pilgrimage in the Holy Land. He congratulated pilgrims who were selected to perform the haj this year and who began their journey early this morning. "I pray that all Malaysian haj pilgrims will have their affairs eased, be blessed with good health, and be protected throughout their time in the Holy Land.

items.title: Injured student in SMK Tanjong Mas car crash moved to regular ward
items.summary: State health director Datuk Dr Mohd Azman Yacob said the victim, Zara Aftar Zamzuri, is scheduled to undergo a corrective procedure on her ear this Monday. "Alhamdulillah, the student was transferred to a regular ward following surgery last week. Dr Mohd Azman spoke to reporters at the inauguration of the new Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) terminal here today.

items.title: Police: Losses from investment scams hit RM1.47 bln last year
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Investment scams caused losses of RM1.47 billion last year, involving 9,603 cases, with the Royal Malaysia Police identifying five main methods, most using social media as the primary channel. Bukit Aman Commercial Crime Investigation Department director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said tactics uncovered included fake companies (clone firms), promises of quick high returns, Ponzi schemes, love scams, and bogus crypto platforms. Rusdi said non-existent investment cases rose sharply from 2024 to March this year, with 6,337 cases causing RM848.6 million losses in 2024, surging to 9,603 cases and RM1.47 billion in 2025.

items.title: Haj season begins: First group of Malaysian pilgrims leave for Medina amid Middle East tensions
items.summary: SEPANG, April 18 — The first group of 284 Malaysian haj pilgrims departed for the Holy Land early this morning from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1 at 4.05 am on Malaysia Airlines flight MH8050. The group comprised 141 and 143 pilgrims from the Alor Setar, Kedah and Sepang departure stations, respectively. “This morning, we saw off 284 of our haj pilgrims.

items.title: Hishammuddin, Khairy among thousands back in Umno, party supreme council opens door for online membership
items.summary: MELAKA, April 18 — Former Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Hishammuddin Hussein and former Umno Youth chief Khairy Jamaluddin were among those who were accepted back into Umno under the Gagasan Rumah Bangsa initiative. Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said the matter was decided at the Umno Supreme Council meeting chaired by its president, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi here last night. The meeting was also attended by Melaka Umno state liaison chairman Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh.

items.title: From ‘just-in-time’ to ‘just-in-case’: Countries rethink economic models as global risks rise, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Countries are moving away from efficiency-driven economic models towards resilience, as rising geopolitical uncertainty reshapes the global economic landscape, including trade and investment strategies. Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said for decades, global economic expansion had been underpinned by geopolitical stability, allowing businesses to optimise operations and maximise efficiency. However, he said that as geopolitical conditions become increasingly unpredictable and businesses compete to reposition themselves, that approach is no longer sufficient.

items.title: Private sector key to ensuring Madani Economy delivers real impact, says Anwar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Greater involvement of the private sector and entrepreneurs is vital to ensure the Madani Economy delivers meaningful, sustainable and widely shared growth, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said the Madani Economy framework sets out efforts to raise the country’s growth ceiling, uplift the standard of living and drive reforms, particularly in strengthening governance. Earlier, Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, chaired a meeting with Bumiputera investment institutions to refine more effective and targeted measures to advance the Bumiputera empowerment agenda in line with the Madani Economy framework.

items.title: Putrajaya exploring strategic fuel reserves as import reliance raises vulnerability, says deputy economy minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — The government is studying a proposal to create strategic petroleum reserves as part of the country’s long-term energy security planning, following global supply concerns. Deputy Economy Minister Datuk Mohd Shahar Abdullah said the proposal was among the matters the government was discussing as it assessed long-term preparedness following the supply disruption that closed the strategic Strait of Hormuz route for 48 days. “The matter related to strategic reserves is indeed being looked at by the government, especially in assessing long-term needs following the crisis that has occurred,” he said on Bernama TV’s Ruang Bicara programme titled “Krisis Tenaga Global: Ekonomi Teruji” today.

items.title: MACC: Top officials from Survey and Mapping Dept to face charges over RM900,000 fund abuse
items.summary: Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said the two are expected to be charged on Wednesday or Thursday. Meanwhile, Azam said MACC has recovered more than 70 per cent of 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) assets, amounting to RM30 billion out of RM42 billion. On Tuesday, MACC said the four artworks brought back are Composition (1953) by Joan Miró; Maison de rendez-vous de chasse de Henri IV, Rue St. Vincent, Montmartre (1934) by Maurice Utrillo; Etude pour femme couchée (1948) by Balthus; and L’Ecuyere et les clowns (1961) by Pablo Picasso.

items.title: Seven Kajang students to face school action after bullying video goes viral
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Seven secondary school students in Kajang will receive disciplinary action by school authorities after being involved in a bullying incident that went viral on social media yesterday. Kajang district police chief ACP Naazron Abdul Yusof said the police found a 47-second video depicting a group of students allegedly verbally bullying a female student. The school has also taken appropriate action against the seven students involved,” he said in a statement here yesterday.

items.title: No approval given: UMS rejects claims of involvement in engineering, biomedical conferences
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 18 — Universiti Malaysia Sabah (UMS) today denied involvement in organising a conference that allegedly used the university’s name unlawfully and promoted UMS as the organiser and venue for the programme without permission on several websites. Its Corporate Communication Centre said that the website promotes the International Global Multidisciplinary Engineering Conference (IGMEC) and the International Conference on Biomedical Engineering & Instrumentation (ICBEI), which falsely claim the university’s involvement. “The university authorities also urge the public to report any such activities or suspicious use of the UMS name to the university for verification and immediate action.

items.title: MOF explains why fuel is getting pricier: Insurance up 17 times, shipping costs triple amid West Asia conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Today’s surge in fuel prices is not solely due to higher crude oil prices following the West Asia conflict, but also to increased risks to the global petroleum supply chain, driving up shipping costs and insurance premiums. “Shipping costs have also jumped threefold, driven by rising oil prices and longer travel routes to avoid conflict areas,” the ministry said in a post on its social media page Friday. It said rising costs along the petroleum supply chain and refining, which far exceed the increase in crude oil prices, ultimately influenced retail prices at the pump.

items.title: Suspects shaping public perception to dilute cases, MACC chief says in ‘narrative war’ warning
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Attempts to distort public perception and undermine institutions are emerging as a key challenge in Malaysia’s fight against corruption. Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Azam Baki said the media now plays a critical role in countering such narratives. “They create doubt, influence public perception, and repeat unfounded claims until they appear as truth, while factual information is drowned out,” he said at the MACC’s sixth media appreciation event last night.

items.title: In an emergency, don’t Google — dial 999 for faster help
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — In a medical emergency, every second counts; but many Malaysians still waste precious time searching for a hospital number instead of dialling the fastest lifeline available: 999. A single call to 999 immediately activates Malaysia’s integrated emergency response system, dispatching the nearest available assistance – often faster, and at no cost. Why call 999?

items.title: Air rifles, jihad books seized at home of Ulu Tiram police station attacker’s family, witness tells High Court
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — A police officer told the High Court here, today, that he seized four air rifles, nine Polyvinyl Chloride (PVC) pipes and several books, related to jihad, from the family home of the Ulu Tiram police station attacker in Johor. ASP Cassidy Banta, 42, said the items were seized when he led a search at the home of Radin Luqman Radin Imran, 21, in Kampung Sungai Tiram, following the May 17, 2024 attack which killed two police personnel. The fifth prosecution witness said that, upon arriving at the house, the door was opened by the first accused, Radin Imran Radin Mohd Yassin, 64, who is also the attacker’s father.

items.title: Health Ministry earmarks RM3.42m to improve Batu Pahat clinics
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, April 17 — The Ministry of Health (MOH) has announced a special allocation of RM3.421 million to upgrade physical facilities and procure medical assets at four clinics in the Batu Pahat district. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the allocation comprised RM500,000 for the Parit Raja Health Clinic, Sri Gading Health Clinic (RM950,000), Parit Yaani Health Clinic (RM1.268 million) and RM685,000 for the Tongkang Pechah Health Clinic. Also present were Health deputy director-general (public health) Dr Ismuni Bohari, state Health director Dr Mohtar Pungut and Sri Gading MP Aminolhuda Hassan.

items.title: MOH sets up task force to study nursing allowance increase
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, April 17 — The Health Ministry (MoH) will discuss the proposed increase in post-basic nursing allowances through an inter-ministerial task force to be established soon. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said the discussions would adopt a whole-of-government approach, involving relevant ministries, including the Finance Ministry. He said the ministry has taken note of the matter, and preliminary discussions have already been held by the MoH task force.

items.title: MACC appoints Hafaz Nazar as investigations chief, Karunanithy promoted to MACA head
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 17 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today announced the appointment and promotions of two senior officers effective today as part of efforts to strengthen the commission’s leadership structure and improve efficiency of its service delivery. The MACC said that Datuk Mohd Hafaz Nazar has been appointed as MACC Investigations Division senior director from his previous position of Malaysian Anti-Corruption Academy (MACA) senior director while Datuk Karunanithy Y Subbiah has been promoted to MACC senior director, from his previous position as Penang MACC director. “The appointments and promotions involving officers who have shown excellent performance, high commitment and integrity in conducting their duties throughout their period of service,” the commission said in a statement today.

items.title: Maid trafficking ring busted in Klang Valley, eight Indonesian women rescued
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 17 — The Immigration Department busted a syndicate involved in exploiting foreign domestic workers and rescued eight Indonesian women believed to be victims in raids at 11 locations around the Klang Valley on April 15. Zakaria said two local women, believed to be illegal agents and identified as a mother and daughter known as “Ibu Maznah”, were arrested in the operation. He said the agents were believed to have supplied and exploited Indonesian women as foreign domestic workers.

items.title: Malaysia to arm border agency with pistols or tasers following Bukit Kayu Hitam shooting
items.summary: PORT KLANG, April 17 — The government has agreed to equip the Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (AKPS) with necessary armaments, with procurement to begin before year-end, said Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. He said the matter had been discussed in meetings with the ministry, and the Cabinet had agreed to the need, following a shooting incident involving the AKPS Bukit Kayu Hitam commander, Mohd Nasaruddin Mohd Nasir, in February. There are various options, such as taser guns or light weapons like pistols.

items.title: Putrajaya rejects report of Sebatik land loss, says Malaysia gained 662ha in broader border deal
items.summary: The rebuttal follows a report on April 15, which quoted the Chief of Staff of the Indonesian President's Office, Muhammad Qodari, as saying that 127.3 hectares on Pulau Sebatik, previously in Malaysia, was now officially Indonesian territory, with Malaysia receiving 4.9 hectares in return. On Pulau Sebatik, an area of 123 hectares previously within Malaysia is now within Indonesia, while Malaysia gained five hectares from Indonesia. At Sg Sinapad and Sg Sesai, Malaysia gained an additional 780 hectares that were previously Indonesian territory.

items.title: After Wangsa Maju scandal, FT Dept says MPPWP is a welfare body and not a business entity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 17 — The Federal Territories Department (JWP) has issued a stern public reminder that the Federal Territories Residents Representative Council (MPPWP) is not a business entity, following a recent viral video scandal allegedly showing two of its members demanding ‘protection money’ from a business here. The clarification comes after a controversy in March 2026, when CCTV-style footage emerged showing two MPPWP members from the Wangsa Maju branch allegedly extorting a massage centre. The department explicitly said that MPPWP members are prohibited from misusing the organisation's identity for personal or business gain.

items.title: Zahid Hamidi: Election readiness primary goal for Melaka Umno meet tonight
items.summary: MELAKA, April 17 — Planning and preparations for the upcoming General Election (GE) and State Elections will be the primary focus of the Umno Melaka meeting scheduled for tonight, said party president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi. "This is the first of the party's meetings in the state as part of our preparations for the upcoming elections," he said during the simultaneous opening ceremony of Umno Branch Meetings for Melaka at the Melaka International Trade Centre (MITC) here today. Also present were Umno Secretary-General Datuk Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki, Umno Youth Chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh, and Melaka Umno Liaison Committee Chairman Datuk Seri Ab Rauf Yusoh.

items.title: Anwar orders study on restoring Langkawi’s duty-free status, to review EV policies following BYD plant concerns
items.summary: CYBERJAYA, April 17 — The government will study the proposal to restore Langkawi’s duty-free status as a move to revitalise the island’s economy, which is currently facing challenges from rising costs and a decline in domestic tourism appeal. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the proposal deserves attention as Langkawi’s tourism sector has been a bit sluggish lately. Anwar also expressed his appreciation to the Sultan of Kedah, Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin Sultan Badlishah, for the proposal.
